title: Assign an asset
content:
Use this endpoint to assign an asset in the Business Center to a TikTok For Business user that is already in the Business Center. You need to be an Admin user of the Business Center. ## Comparing v1.2 and v1.3 The following table outlines the differences between v1.2 and v1.3 endpoints. ```xtable |Changes{30%}|v1.2{35%}|v1.3{35%}| |---|---|---| |Endpoint path|/v1.2/bc/asset/assign/|/v1.3/bc/asset/assign/| |Request parameter data type |`bc_id`: number
`user_id`: number
`asset_id`: number|`bc_id`: string
`user_id`: string
`asset_id`: string| |New request parameter |/|`store_role`
`business_account_roles`| ``` ## Request **Endpoint** **Method** POST **Header** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {Required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {Required}|string|Request message type
Allowed format: `"application/json"`. | ``` **Parameters** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |bc_id {Required}|string| Business Center ID. | |user_id {Required}|string| The ID of the Business Center user that you want to assign the asset to.
You can obtain the Business Center user ID via `user_id` returned by [/bc/member/get/](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1739939404802049).| |asset_type {Required}|string|Asset type.

Enum values:
  • `ADVERTISER`: ad account (advertiser account).
  • `CATALOG`: catalog.
  • `TIKTOK_SHOP`: TikTok Shop.
  • `LEAD`: Leads.
  • `TT_ACCOUNT`: TikTok account as an ad delivery asset. You can run ads from these TikTok accounts. This asset type corresponds to the identity type `BC_AUTH_TT`. Learn more from [Identities](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738958351620097).
  • `MANAGED_BUSINESS_ACCOUNT`: TikTok account for Business Account management. You can run ads from these TikTok accounts, and manage messages, leads, forms, comments, product links, and insights in your TikTok account.
| |asset_id {Required}|string|Asset ID.
  • If `asset_type` is `ADVERTISER`, this field is the advertiser ID.
  • If `asset_type` is `CATALOG`, this field is the catalog ID.
  • If `asset_type` is `TIKTOK_SHOP`, this field is the TikTok Shop ID.
  • If `asset_type` is `LEAD`, this field is the form library ID. To obtain more details about form libraries that you have access to, use [/page/library/get/](https://business-api.tiktok.com/portal/docs?id=1740029915327489).
  • If `asset_type` is `TT_ACCOUNT`, this field is the ID of a TikTok account as an ad delivery asset in a Business Center.
  • If `asset_type` is `MANAGED_BUSINESS_ACCOUNT`, this field is the ID of a TikTok account for Business Account management in a Business Center.
| |advertiser_role{+Conditional} |string|Required when `asset_type` is `ADVERTISER`.

Advertiser role. This is equivalent to Layer 2 of Ad Account Permissions. Enum values: `ADMIN`, `OPERATOR`, `ANALYST`. For details about these permissions, see [Manage Permissions on TikTok Business Center](https://ads.tiktok.com/help/article?aid=12791).| |catalog_role{+Conditional} |string| Required when `asset_type` is `CATALOG`.

Business Center user's permissions to the catalog.

Enum values:
  • `ADMIN`: The Business Center user has Admin permissions to the catalog.
  • `AD_PROMOTE`: The Business Center user can only use the catalog for promotions.
    • | |form_library_role{+Conditional} |string| Required when `asset_type` is `LEAD`.

    Form library role assigned to the user. Currently, only `ADMIN` is supported.| |tt_account_roles {+Conditional}|string[]|Required when `asset_type` is `TT_ACCOUNT`.

    Business Center user's ad delivery permission to the TikTok account.

    Enum values:
    • `POST`: Existing posts permissions, including accessing your own profile info, accessing your own public videos, and accessing your own ad-authorized videos.
    • `LIVE`: LIVE video permissions (Only available in some regions), including accessing your own profile info and accessing LIVE videos.
    • `DIRECT_MESSAGE`: Direct message permissions, including allowing everyone to send you a direct message and reading and managing direct messages in your inbox, in addition to messaging or interacting with other accounts on your behalf.
    | |business_account_roles {+Conditional}|string[]|Required only when `asset_type` is `MANAGED_BUSINESS_ACCOUNT`.

    Business Center user's Business Account management permission to the TikTok account.

    Enum values:
    • `BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_ADMIN`: Business Account admin. Business Account admins have access to all Business Account features, including verification, member management, and linking to Business Center.
    • `BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_OPERATOR`: Business Account operator. Business Account operators can manage TikTok analytics, posts, messages, and profile information.
    • `BUSINESS_ACCOUNT_ANALYST`: Business Account analyst. Business Account analysts only have access to analytics and are not able to manage posts, messages, etc.

    Max size: 1.| |store_role{+Conditional} |string|Required when `asset_type` is `TIKTOK_SHOP`.

    Business Center user's permission to a TikTok Shop.

    Enum values:
    • `AD_PROMOTION`: The Business Center user can only use the TikTok Shop for promotions.
    | ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code cURL cURL) curl --location --request POST 'business-api.tiktok.com/open_api/v1.3/bc/asset/assign/' \ --header 'Access-Token: {{Access-Token}}' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data-raw '{ "bc_id": "{{bc_id}}", "user_id": "{{user_id}}", "asset_type": "ADVERTISER", "asset_id": "{{asset_id}}", "advertiser_role": "ADMIN" }' (/code) ``` ## Response ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |code |number|Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |message |string|Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).

    Note: If the response code is `40002`, and the response message is "Authorization roles exceed the role range that the BC can manage.", then you need to check whether the value you pass in for `advertiser_role`, `catalog_role`, `form_library_role`, or `tt_account_roles` in the request is allowed.| |request_id|string| Unique ID of the request.| |data| object | Returned data.| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code cURL cURL) HTTPS/1.1 200 OK { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "{{request_id}}", "data": {} } (/code) ```
